summary_lines: Newcastle tender was re-released after AGFA challenged Sectra's original win in December 2024, causing the tender to be dropped.
New tender requirements are significantly more extensive and stringent.
Submission made on 2 September. Awaiting Trust decision — timeline unknown.
Deal value: £7,600,000. Rep: Leigh Betts.

Opportunity for NHS Buckinghamshire, Oxfordshire and Berkshire West Integrated Care Board — covers 6 Trusts: Oxford University NHS FT, Oxford Health NHS FT, Berkshire Healthcare NHS FT, Royal Berkshire NHS FT, Buckinghamshire Healthcare NHS Trust, and South Central Ambulance Service NHS FT.
Engaged following ECR and UKIO; region mostly Insignia/Intelerad — no current Sectra sites, representing a significant greenfield opportunity.
Pre-Market Engagement released; response deadline extended to 15 December.
Scenarios show poor strategic alignment (heavy on-prem, separate solutions).
Tender originally expected 23 February 2026 — as of 13 March 2026, no tender received and radio silence.
Deal value: £10,750,000. Rep: Leigh Betts.
Sectra secured opportunity, beating AGFA and Philips.
Covers 7 NHS Trusts (4 Sectra — Whittington, North Midds, Moorfields and GOSH; 3 Non-Sectra — UCLH, Royal Free and RNOH).
Radiology + Breast Screening only for now.
Additional Ophthalmology VNA opportunity discussions ongoing with Kevan Turnbull (EA for Moorfields).
Additional options pricing provided, waiting for further updates from NCL.
Contract expected now by end of the year.
LNW and C&W migrating from Sectra on-premise to Sectra One Cloud.
Region includes Hillingdon and Imperial (CD: Anastasia Gontsarova) â€“ aim will be to consolidate across the region eventually as these contracts mature.
Going back to drawing board with Contracts as single contract preferred.
Deployment commenced on the back of signed Letters of Guarantee (covering full cost of MS Reservation Period).
CCN issued for warranty extension to align with new contract Go-Live.
last_update_lines: Contract fully signed! Will remove this from next week on.

summary_lines: NPIC Contract coming to an end in September 2027.
They have requested various extension and reprofile options to accommodate for the increased licencing profile they are expecting.
Option include extending for 2.5yrs to align with the Expo-e contract and fully utilised the procured storage.
They have also requested a Sectra One Cloud option to evaluate moving to SaaS in 2028.
last_update_lines: CCN signed - will remove next week.

west midlands imaging network (wmin) â€“.display_name: West Midlands Imaging Network (WMIN) â€“ Converged Digital Imaging Platform (CDIP)
summary_lines: The WMIN, led by University Hospitals Birmingham, has issued an RFI for a regional Converged Digital Imaging Platform.
Aims to unify PACS/RIS across 15 Trusts, supporting shared reporting, AI, and data analytics.
Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust
Sandwell and West Birmingham Hospitals NHS Trust
The Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust
The Dudley Group NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust
The Royal Orthopaedic Hospital NHS Foundation Trust
Birmingham Womenâ€™s and Childrenâ€™s NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals Coventry & Warwickshire NHS Trust
The George Eliot Hospital NHS Trust
South Warwickshire University NHS Trust
Worcestershire Acute Hospitals NHS Trust
Wye Valley NHS Trust
The Shrewsbury & Telford Hospital NHS Trust
The Robert Jones & Agnes Hunt Orthopaedic Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals of North Midlands NHS Trust
Estimated value: £76 m (5+5 or 10-year contract).
Tender expected 2026 following current RFI and market testing.
Currently multi-vendor landscape; move toward single platform.
Sectra well positioned given regional footprint and proven success in similar multi-Trust imaging programmes (e.g. GM, NIPACS+).
Competition expected from incumbent PACS suppliers; early engagement critical.
last_update_lines: Response submitted - will remove this entry from next week while we wait for tender.

summary_lines: Royal Marsden requires a hardware refresh in 2026.
Currently on customer-provided hardware — evaluating options between an on-premises refresh, a customer-provided cloud refresh, or a Sectra One Cloud refresh for the remainder of the contract.
Meeting scheduled for 6 June. Deal value: £1,300,000. Rep: Guil Carvalho.
The North East and North Cumbria region (formerly NECA) has gone out to tender for a new Digital Pathology solution.

Currently a Sectra DPAT site comprising: County Durham and Darlington NHSFT, Gateshead Health NHSFT, Newcastle Upon Tyne Hospitals NHSFT, North Tees and Hartlepool NHSFT, South Tees Hospitals NHSFT, Northumbria Healthcare NHSFT, and North Cumbria Integrated Care NHSFT.
Consortium is on customer-provided hardware and was mandated to retender despite broad satisfaction with Sectra's service.
This was Sectra UK's first live DPAT site (October 2019) and was very competitively priced at the time.
Deal value: £1,400,000. Rep: Guil Carvalho.
Royal Free Breast Screening is being set up as an interim destination for breast screening data migrated from InHealth (ONEL), following the end of InHealth's contract.
This interim deployment will ultimately fold into the broader NCL project.
This is a Sectra-to-Sectra migration acting as a stopgap until the broader NCL project goes live.
Hardware has already been built; no signed contract yet — an SLA has been sent and is awaiting customer sign-off.
Deal value: ~£500k. Rep: Guil Carvalho.
Barts Health contract is expiring next year.
Evaluating options to join NEL Sectra One Cloud — considering hybrid cloud options ahead of a potential merge.
Current option under evaluation: 1-year migration with hardware in situ, plus 4.5-year contract to join NEL Sectra One Cloud.
Barts seems dead set on moving along with a standalone Sectra One Cloud SaaS Option.
National Digital Pathology solution to be deployed phased across 40+ labs in the Republic of Ireland (HSE).
Sectra has been shortlisted in the last five — no further progress communicated since shortlisting.
NIMIS (National Radiology system, delivered by Change/Optum) is not expected to go to tender soon despite questions about the legality of contract extensions.
PIN released — expected to have a heavy on-premises focus. On-prem + scanners + workstations represents poor strategic fit.
Tender released 13th August. Working on submission with Dell/PFH/Leica and Sysmex.
Competitors expected to be large System Integrators/MES providers/Consultancy Firms.

Intel gathered states the 5 shortlisted bids and scores: EY + Roche (885pts), Sectra/Dell/PFH/Leica (840pts), Siemens + Proscia + PwC (TBC), GE + Tribvn + Deloitte (740pts), Optum (Change) + Gestalt Diagnostic (726pts).
Deal value: £20,000,000. Fit: 7.0. Profit: 4.0. Rep: Guil Carvalho.
Pre-tender opportunity covering Cambridge University Hospitals NHS FT and Royal Papworth Hospital NHS FT.
Awaiting tender release — no further detail on record. As of 13 March 2026, no tender received.
Deal value: £9,500,000. Fit: 7.0. Profit: 7.0. Rep: Leigh Betts.
Hull (Humber Health Partnership) is preparing to go out to tender for a new imaging solution.
The partnership brings together Hull University Teaching Hospitals and North Lincolnshire and Goole NHS FT, now collaborating as if a single Trust.
Hull side is unhappy with AGFA — AGFA Enterprise cannot be installed at Hull despite being live at North Lincolnshire and Goole, causing the two systems to be unable to work together.
The partnership has decided not to wait for AGFA to resolve the interoperability issue and will proceed with a tender.
Sectra held a call with Hull during the week of 9 March 2026.
Deal value not yet confirmed. Fit: 8.0. Profit: 7.0. Rep: Leigh Betts.
Meeting held with SWASH on 15 July 2025.
SWASH is looking to procure multiple Sectra modules in a phased approach: PBR by November, RIS in the following year, and full PACS the year after.
Inclusive of all Trusts currently comprising SWASH+: Southampton, Portsmouth, Salisbury, Hampshire and Isle of Wight.
Fit: 9.0. Profit: 8.0. Rep: Guil Carvalho.
